why: why did nested object change after copy
how: how to copy object without reference
how to deep clone in javascript
output: nested array modified in cloned object

1_beginner_explanation.summary: Because `obj2 = obj1` just shares a reference, we need special tools to actually **copy** an object. A **Shallow Copy** (using `{...obj}` or `Object.assign`) creates a new object, but if there are objects *inside* your object, those nested objects are still shared references! A **Deep Copy** creates a completely independent clone, including all nested objects and arrays.
1_beginner_explanation.analogy: Shallow Copy: You photocopy a document. The top-level text is copied. But there is a link to a YouTube video on the page. The link is copied, but it still points to the same video. Deep Copy: You photocopy the document, and you also download the video and put it on a new flash drive. Everything is 100% independent.

2_internal_mechanism.execution_flow: When using the Spread Operator `{ ...obj }`, the engine loops through the top-level keys and copies their values to a new memory address. But if a value is a pointer (an array/object), it just copies the pointer. Modern JS introduced `structuredClone(obj)` which recursively walks down every nested level, allocating brand new memory for every single piece.
2_internal_mechanism.memory: Shallow copies save memory but risk mutation bugs. Deep copies use more memory but guarantee safety from side-effects.

3_real_world_example.use_case: Safely copying user settings where some settings are nested inside an array.

4_common_confusion.trap: Relying on the old hack `JSON.parse(JSON.stringify(obj))` to do deep cloning. While it works for basic data, it completely strips out Functions, Dates, undefined, and Maps/Sets.
4_common_confusion.fix: Stop using the JSON hack. In modern JavaScript (2022+), always use `structuredClone(obj)` for deep copies.

6_edge_case.quirk: Neither `structuredClone` nor the JSON hack can clone functions or DOM nodes. If an object has methods on it, deep cloning it will result in an error or strip the methods away.
